Tamil Nadu.heading: The Timeless Dravidian Legacy of Tamil Nadu
Tamil Nadu.introduction: Tamil Nadu is a land of ancient temples, classical arts, and one of the world’s oldest living cultures. From towering Dravidian architecture to Bharatanatyam and rich literary traditions, the state offers a deeply immersive journey into South India’s enduring heritage.

items.title: Meenakshi Temple
items.description: The Meenakshi Amman Temple in Madurai is a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture, known for its towering gopurams and intricate sculptures. Dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi, it is a major pilgrimage site. The temple reflects centuries of artistic and spiritual tradition.

items.title: Brihadeeswara Temple
items.description: Built by the Chola dynasty, Brihadeeswara Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its massive scale and precision. Its towering vimana and detailed carvings highlight advanced engineering. The temple stands as a symbol of Tamil architectural brilliance.

items.title: Bharatanatyam Dance
items.description: Bharatanatyam is one of India’s oldest classical dance forms, originating in Tamil Nadu’s temples. Known for its expressive gestures and rhythmic footwork, it tells stories from mythology. The dance is a cornerstone of Tamil cultural identity.

items.title: Kolam Art
items.description: Kolam is a traditional form of drawing geometric patterns at the entrances of homes using rice flour. It is both decorative and symbolic, representing prosperity and harmony. The practice reflects everyday artistic expression in Tamil culture.

items.title: Tamil Cuisine
items.description: Tamil cuisine is known for its diverse flavors, including dishes like dosa, idli, and sambar. Meals are often served on banana leaves and emphasize balance and nutrition. The cuisine reflects both tradition and regional variation.

items.title: Tamil Language
items.description: Tamil is one of the oldest continuously used languages in the world, with a literary tradition spanning over two millennia. It is a classical language with rich poetry, philosophy, and history. Tamil remains central to the identity of the state.
